_note: ORIGINAL CS Navigator content added to align Learn with the existing Part 2 quiz category. No source prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Debug: Part 2
summary: Trace mutation, aliases, boundaries, and multi-step state.
refresher: Deeper Python bugs often begin before the failing line. Track object identity when data is shared, inspect types beside values, and test empty, one-item, and boundary inputs.

blocks.body: Deeper Python bugs often begin before the failing line. Track object identity when data is shared, inspect types beside values, and test empty, one-item, and boundary inputs. Trace mutation, aliases, boundaries, and multi-step state.

blocks.caption: The shallow list copy gives the two variables independent outer lists for this simple data.
blocks.code:
original = [1, 2]
copy = original.copy()
copy.append(3)
print(len(original))
print(len(copy))

blocks.title: Track the state
blocks.body: Assignment alone creates another reference to the same list. A mutation through either name then appears through both.

blocks.prompt: What does `b = a` do for a list?
blocks.why: Both names refer to one list object, so later mutations are shared.
Negative-one indexing still requires at least one element, so an empty list raises IndexError.
